title: Sync with Other Pocket Operators
description: Connect multiple Pocket Operators together so they play in perfect sync.

steps.title: Gather Your Equipment
steps.instruction: You'll need: two or more Pocket Operators, stereo 3.5mm cables (one less than the number of POs), and optionally a splitter cable for monitoring.
steps.tip: Make sure to use stereo (TRS) cables, not mono (TS) cables. Mono cables won't carry the sync signal properly.

steps.title: Set Master PO to SY1
steps.instruction: On the PO that will be the master (controls tempo): Hold the BPM knob and press pad 1 to set sync mode to SY1 (sync output).
steps.tip: The master PO controls the tempo - other units will follow its BPM.

steps.title: Set Slave PO to SY2
steps.instruction: On the PO that will follow (slave): Hold BPM knob and press pad 2 to set sync mode to SY2 (sync input).
steps.tip: If you have more than 2 POs, set middle units to SY3 (pass-through) and only the last one to SY2.

steps.title: Connect the Cable
steps.instruction: Connect a stereo cable from the master PO's sync port to the slave PO's sync port.
steps.warning: The sync port carries both clock (left channel) and audio (right channel). Audio from the master will be heard through the slave's output.

steps.title: Start Playback
steps.instruction: Press play on the master PO. The slave PO should start automatically and stay locked to the master's tempo. Adjust BPM on the master - slave follows!
steps.tip: If sync isn't working, double-check that both units are in the correct sync modes and using a stereo cable.

outcomes: Successfully synced two or more Pocket Operators
